en.extract:
The citrine canary-flycatcher (Culicicapa helianthea) is a species of bird in the family Stenostiridae. The term citrine refers to its yellowish colouration.  It is found in Sulawesi and the Philippines. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.
It has been observed gathering in mixed-species flocks, with species including Everett's white-eyes, black-naped monarchs, orange-bellied flowerpeckers, elegant tits, crimson sunbirds, garden sunbirds, and Negros striped babblers.

The '''citrine canary-flycatcher''' ('''''Culicicapa helianthea''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Stenostiridae]]. The term [[Citrine (colour)|''citrine'']] refers to its yellowish colouration.  It is found in [[Sulawesi]] and the [[Philippines]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

It has been observed gathering in mixed-species flocks, with species including [[Everett's white-eye|Everett's white-eyes]], [[Black-naped monarch|black-naped monarchs]], [[Orange-bellied flowerpecker|orange-bellied flowerpeckers]], [[Elegant tit|elegant tits]], [[Crimson sunbird|crimson sunbirds]], [[Garden sunbird|garden sunbirds]],<ref>{{cite journal|author=Paguntalan, LISA MARIE J.|name-list-style= and |author2=Philip Godfrey Jakosalem |title=Significant records of birds in forests on Cebu island, central Philippines|journal=Forktail|volume= 24|year=2008|pages= 48–56}}</ref> and [[Negros striped babbler|Negros striped babblers]].<ref>{{cite journal|author=ORACION, JEAN HENRI M.|display-authors= et al. |title=Notes on the distribution and behaviour of the Negros Striped Babbler ''Zosterornis nigrorum'' in Cuernos de Negros, Philippines|journal=Journal of Asian Ornithology |volume=38 |year=2022|pages=113–118}}</ref>
